title: The fundamental advantages of human vs AI
html:
For all the speed and breadth of modern AI, one thing that AI models as software fall short of compared to the human brain is that this brain “wetware” is equipped with a biological advantage of elasticity. The mind is not a fixed machine running fixed weights; it reshapes itself in the very act of being used.

AI models do not learn continuously. Once trained, their weights are frozen until someone deliberately retrains them. There’s no resemblance to the “neurons that fire together, wire together” capability of the mind — that quiet, ongoing rewiring that happens whether or not we notice.

We have a complicated biochemical machine built in that “fine-tunes” with every inference. Each time we think, recall, or struggle with something, information patterns are encoded and abstracted into efficient chunks automatically. Consider how a phone number you dialed a hundred times becomes a single fluid gesture rather than ten separate digits — that compression happens on its own. LLM models, by contrast, start from scratch no matter how much time they spend dealing with the same problem; nothing from one conversation settles into the next.

The difference runs deeper than memory. The agentic behavior of LLMs acquires contextual connections through wording semantic similarity — matching things that sound alike. The human mind understands and creates connections based on abstracted relations, effectively performing at a higher dimensional level of thinking, linking ideas by what they mean rather than merely how they are phrased. (A distinction I keep returning to from a friend’s thinking.)
